title: Krath Holy Crusade
content:

Tales of the Jedi – The Sith War]]'' |next=[[Cleansing of the Nine Houses]]''[[Knights of the Old Republic Campaign Guide]]'' |image=[[File:Krath_warrior_and_droid.jpg]] |name=Krath Holy Crusade victory **Dissolution of the [[Krath/Legends|Krath]] **Redemption of [[Ulic Qel-Droma/Legends|Ulic Qel-Droma]] **[[Exar Kun/Legends|Exar Kun]] is removed as a galactic threat |battles=*[[Political uprising of the Empress Teta system]] *[[Battle of Koros Major]] *[[Battle of Deneba]] *[[Mission to Empress Teta]] *[[Duel in the Iron Citadel (Satal Keto)|Duel in the Iron Citadel]] *[[Battle of Empress Teta|Second Battle of Empress Teta]] *[[Duel in the Iron Citadel (Exar Kun)|Second Duel in the Iron Citadel]] |side1=*Jedi Order *Galactic Republic |side2=[[Krath/Legends|Krath]] |side3= |side4= |commanders1=*[[Jedi Master/Legends|Jedi Master]] [[Arca Jeth]]† *Jedi Master [[Thon]] *Jedi Master [[Vodo-Siosk Baas]]† *[[Jedi Knight/Legends|Jedi Knight]] [[Nomi Sunrider]] *Jedi Knight [[Ulic Qel-Droma/Legends|Ulic Qel-Droma]] {{C|[[Duel in the Iron Citadel (Satal Keto)|Defected]] to the Krath, and later [[Battle of Ossus (Great Sith War)|returned]] to the Jedi}} *[[Admiral/Legends|Admiral]] [[Orley Vanicus]] |commanders2=*[[Aleema Keto]]† *[[Satal Keto/Legends|Satal Keto]]† *[[Ulic Qel-Droma/Legends|Ulic Qel-Droma]] {{C|[[Duel in the Iron Citadel (Satal Keto)|Left]] the Jedi, and later [[Battle of Ossus (Great Sith War)|abandoned]] the Krath}} |commanders3= |commanders4= }} {{Quote|Master Arca… despite all the appeals of the Galactic Senate, the brutal atrocities continue in the Empress Teta system…"
"Yes… the Krath have unleashed the dark side with ''great'' malevolence.|[[Ulic Qel-Droma/Legends|Ulic Qel-Droma]] and [[Arca Jeth]]|''[[Star Wars: Tales of the Jedi – Dark Lords of the Sith]]''}} The '''Krath Holy Crusade''' ([[3997 BBY|3997]]-[[3996 BBY]]) was a series of battles fought before and during the [[Great Sith War/Legends|Great Sith War]], and comprised the bulk of that conflict. The crusade was a culmination of events that began with a violent [[Political uprising of the Empress Teta system|regime change]] in the [[Empress Teta system/Legends|Empress Teta system]] staged by [[Aleema Keto|Aleema]] and [[Satal Keto/Legends|Satal Keto]], heirs to the [[Empress Teta/Legends|Tetan]] monarchy and also leaders of the [[Sith/Legends|Sith]]-inspired [[Krath/Legends|Krath]] cult. After the war, the Krath order constructed a series of temples on several of the [[Planet/Legends|worlds]] they had seized during the Crusade. This Crusade started as the [[Mandalorian Crusaders|Mandalorians]] began their holy crusade against the [[Basiliskan]]s on [[Basilisk]], and ended after the [[Battle of Yavin 4 (Great Sith War)|defeat]] of [[Dark Lord of the Sith/Legends|Dark Lord of the Sith]] [[Exar Kun/Legends|Exar Kun]] on [[Yavin 4/Legends|Yavin 4]]. ==Origins== ===Dark side awakening=== {{Quote|That planet! Onderon…they still practice Sith magic there!''"
"''Of course That's where we can unlock the secrets of this book!''""
"''Let's get our ship, Satal. We're going to Onderon.|Satal and Aleema make plans to head for Onderon|[[Tales of the Jedi: Dark Lords of the Sith (audio drama)|''Tales of the Jedi: Dark Lords of the Sith'' audio drama]]}} In the year [[3998 BBY]], two young nobles from the [[Empress Teta system/Legends|Empress Teta system]] arrived on [[Coruscant/Legends|Coruscant]]. Satal Keto and his cousin Aleema, the heirs to the [[Empress Teta/Legends|Tetan]] monarchy, were on a vacation of sorts to the Galactic capital. Upon arriving on the capital world in their royal yacht dubbed the ''[[Krath Enchanter]]'', the Ketos started their trip by visiting Coruscant's [[Galactic Museum/Legends|Galactic Museum]]. In truth, they had come to Coruscant in search of knowledge of the ancient [[Sith/Legends|Sith]], and more specifically, for anything related to the dark side. At the time of the Ketos' arrival, the museum had nearly completed a new dark side-based exhibit, appropriately titled ''"Dark Lords of the Sith"''. The Ketos begged to be allowed to explore the incomplete exhibit, but the curator was hesitant due to explicit instructions from the [[Jedi Order/Legends|Jedi Order]] that the hall be off limits until a sufficient security detail was in place.[[Tales of the Jedi (audio drama)|''Tales of the Jedi'' audio drama]] The Ketos continued to beg, and finally the curator relented. They were then allowed access to a hall dedicated to the Sith was filled with ancient and priceless artifacts, books, tomes, and amulets, all radiating with dark side power; power that Satal and Aleema could feel through the Force. They quickly stole one of the books and fled back to their lodging quarters. Unable to read the book and dejected by that fact, the Ketos' hope was renewed when they heard a news report about [[Dark side adept/Legends|dark side adepts]] on Onderon who practiced ancient [[Sith sorcery/Legends|Sith magic]]. They knew that Onderon was where the secrets of the book had any chance of being revealed to them, and they immediately left Coruscant on a course for the dark side world. ===Violent political regime change=== {{Quote|Well, things seem to be peaceful enough on Onderon now!"
"On Onderon, perhaps. But there has been a political uprising in the Empress Teta system. I suspect that this turmoil is connected to the Freedon Nadd Uprising here in the city of Iziz.|[[Cay Qel-Droma]] and [[Arca Jeth]]|}} Returning to their system, the cousins began a coup to overthrow their parents; they confused everyone with Sith illusions. They then killed their parents by dipping them in molten carbonite. ==The war== ===Last stand of Empress Teta=== {{Quote|Koros Major is the last planet to resist our will, Aleema. This is a test of our power…If the proud soldiers of Koros Major can be confounded by Sith magic, certainly we can take on the Jedi themselves!"
"Check the scanners, Satal…I think you're about to get your chance.|Aleema and Satal Keto, as the Republic fleet drops out of hyperspace|}} The Krath forces launched an attack at the last free planet, Koros Major. Aleema assisted her forces through use of her Sith powers. Luckily, a detachment of Republic forces jumped into Koros Major's space to assist the barely holding planet. But out of nowhere, myths though extinct space grazers appeared. [[Jedi Knight/Legends|Jedi Knight]] [[Nomi Sunrider]] saw through the illusions and told the Republic forces to ignore them and concentrate on the stations. Satal pulled a trump card against the Republic by sending out the chaos fighters. Nomi assumed all of them were illusions and telepathically attacked Aleema, who was providing some of the illusions, distracting her, not realizing most of them are real. The fighters slammed kamikaze into the Republic forces, forcing them to retreat. ===Ambush on Deneba=== {{Quote|It seems these upstart Sith magicians are not content to rule their own planets. Already they try to spread their misery to others.|Vodo-Siosk Baas speaking of the Krath|audio=VodoUpstartSithMagicians-DLOTS.flac|}} Meanwhile, the Krath were enjoying their recent victory over the Republic. Back in their [[Iron Citadel/Legends|Iron Citadel]] stronghold, Satal Keto tended to his loxed out cousin Aleema, who through the power of Nomi Sunrider's psychic attack was rendered comatose.[[Tales of the Jedi – Dark Lords of the Sith 2|''Tales of the Jedi – Dark Lords of the Sith'' 2]] When she finally came around, Satal quickly brought Aleema up to speed on the current situation. He told her that the Jedi and Republic fleet had been driven away, thanks to the power of Sith magic. He also told Aleema of the Jedi convocation being held on Deneba. Satal managed to insert one of his [[Tetan Corsair]]s in orbit around Deneba for the next stage of his plan. At an appointed time of Lord Keto's choosing, the Corsair would deploy its full concomitant of [[Escape pod/Legends|life-pods]], each carrying an assortment of [[Krath war droid/Legends|Krath war droids]]. The second prong of the attack would be on the planet's surface itself; Satal planned to use the Jedi's [[Service droid/Legends|service droids]] against them, supplanting their original programing with that of his own Krath war droids. Once the life-pods were deployed, a signal would be transmitted from the Corsair, activating the service droid's programming trap. Chiming the Corsair [[Captain/Legends|captain]] via [[Comlink/Legends|comlink]], [[Lord/Legends|Lord]] Keto ordered him to begin the assault.[[Tales of the Jedi – Dark Lords of the Sith 3|''Tales of the Jedi – Dark Lords of the Sith'' 3]] The Jedi were just barely able to escape the ambush, even as Cay's [[Service droid/Legends|servant droid]] met a swift demise from the blade of Nomi Sunrider's [[Lightsaber/Legends|lightsaber]]. But Cay's was not the only droid affected by the program trap. All of the Conclave server droids received the Krath transmission, and instantly began attacking their Jedi owners. Cay thanked Nomi while reminding everyone how he warned them of the droids' suspicious behavior. Much to his horror his notions were validated as he and the other Jedi were forced to dodge heavy [[Blaster/Legends|blaster]] fire. On top of that, Krath war droids were emerging from the life-pod wreckage. The Jedi took the battle directly to them, leaving hundreds of lightsaber-cleaved droids lay strewn across the battlefield. Master Jeth demonstrated one of the more subtle uses of [[The Force/Legends|the Force]]; to reach inside of the droids, and disable their inner-workings. Many more droids fell under Master Arca's [[Ionize|droid disabling]] technique, and Ulic complimented the ability. Just as he was speaking, a war droid blindsided him, and prepared to finish him off. But Arca was aware of Ulic's peril and again used the Force to destroy the droid. As he diverted his attention to speak to Qel-Droma, one of the downed droids activated its secondary systems, and fired a blaster shot into Jeth's back, felling the Jedi Master. Standing behind him was the war droid he just saved Ulic from, blaster still raised and smoking. As the droid mocked them, Qel-Droma gave into his rage and vanquished the droid. He then rushed to his Master's side, to hear Arca's last words of wisdom and encouragement; to fight the dark side, not align with it. Arca told his former apprentice that he had a weapon that the darkness could not withstand: the power of the Light. He then became one with the Force, leaving only his [[Jedi apparel/Legends|Jedi robes]] behind. ====The corruption of Ulic Qel-Droma==== ====Jedi defector==== Convinced that the only way to stop the Krath is to get inside them, Ulic proposed he personally infiltrate them. Though the Jedi Masters were appalled at the idea, they reluctantly allowed him to try it out. But before he left, Master [[Odan-Urr/Legends|Odan-Urr]] showed him, through a Sith holocron, how the Sith spend their lives—literally. Qel-Droma's arrival in Cinnagar did not go unnoticed; the Krath were well aware of his presence. He was prepared to kill the young Jedi but Aleema just told him to wait. The next day, she was ready to execute a prisoner when the crowd began rioting. ===Qel-Droma's rescue=== Worried about Ulic, Nomi spearheaded a mission to Cinnagar to rescue him. She allowed herself to be captured, just as Ulic was helping the Tetans advance their technologies. Suspicious, Satal had Nomi brought before Ulic and was going to execute her, but Qel-Droma said that he would do it himself the next morning to maintain his cover. He sent a service droid with a message still detailing his mission to Nomi; however, it was intercepted by Satal. Furious at this betrayal, Satal ordered his servant Norgor to kill Ulic and have Nomi brought to him. Ulic was not caught off guard after sleeping with Aleema, though. He learned of Satal planned attack at Deneba. Enraged, he decided to search for Satal as Nomi escaped the dungeons. ====Death of Lord Keto==== Satal merely taunted Ulic as the two fought, only to be slain by Ulic who had submitted to his anger.
